# Phase 6 — doc-pipeline risk report

Generated by `tests/harness/doc-pipeline-harness.test.js` against the mocked LLM.
Ranked worst-first by overall health score. See the legend at the bottom for what the columns mean.

Run configuration:
- Doc types: 28
- Jurisdictions per doc_type: 3 (nigeria, usa, uk)
- Samples per (doc_type × jurisdiction): 5 (clean, truncated, missing-required-clause, title-drift, forbidden-combination)
- Total mocked generations: 420

## Legend

- **Clean pass rate** — of the clean samples expected to generate a valid document, how many landed 200 OK? Ideal: `3/3` (one per jurisdiction).
- **Negative-case catch rate** — of the malformed samples (truncated / missing clause / title drift / forbidden combination) expected to fail-close, how many were actually caught? Ideal: all applicable negatives caught. `n/a` rows indicate registry gaps: the doc_type has no `required_clauses`, no `forbidden_titles`, and no `forbidden_combinations` configured, so the malformed samples have nothing to hit.
- **Registry gaps** — columns of the registry entry that are empty arrays. Founder-facing input for next-week registry-population work. DO NOT auto-populate from this column.

## Not done here (by Phase 6 scope)

- Live-canary run against real Anthropic
- Observability dashboards / fallback-rate alerting
- Hand-populating `required_clauses` / `forbidden_titles` for the other 26 doc_types — that is a separate workstream.
